user_input:
Key Aspects of Human Memory Storage:
Encoding & Storage: Memories are encoded (translated into neural signals) and stored by strengthening specific synapses between neurons, often described as "neurons that fire together, wire together".
Brain Regions:
Hippocampus: Located in the temporal lobe, it acts as an indexer, critical for forming new episodic (autobiographical) memories.
Neocortex: Long-term memories are stored here in a distributed fashion.
Amygdala: Assigns emotional significance to memories.
Prefrontal Cortex: Involved in short-term memory.
Types of Memory Storage:
Short-Term/Working Memory: Temporary storage (similar to RAM) lasting roughly 20-30 seconds, often kept in "neural loops" in the prefrontal cortex.
Long-Term Memory: Permanent storage created by structural changes in the brain over time.
Non-Brain Cell Memory: Recent research indicates that cells outside the brain, such as in nerve or kidney tissues, can also store memories by activating specific genes in response to repeated patterns, similar to neurons. 
Memory Consolidation is the process of strengthening these neural connections, converting short-term memories into long-term ones, often occurring through repeated exposure or emotional impact

user_input:
Multi-user awareness with separate profiles, so she can track different people instead of treating everyone the same.
Face detection and face recognition so she can try to identify who is at the computer.
Automatic camera polling, so she does not need a manual snapshot every time to check who is there.
Presence awareness, using webcam visibility and recent interaction to estimate whether someone is actually around.
“Unknown speaker” handling, so if someone is typing and no face is visible, she can treat identity as unknown instead of pretending she knows.
Memory storage with retrieval, so she can recall relevant past information instead of only using the current message.
Reflection storage separated from normal memory, so reflections can be logged, tagged, recalled later, and promoted into memory only when they matter.
Full-message saving support, so Ava can intentionally store an entire user message when she thinks it is important.
Percentage-based memory importance instead of only labels like medium or high.
Ability to re-evaluate memory importance later and raise or lower it.
Goal and curiosity systems, so she can create her own goals or things she wants to find out.
Weighted memory scoring based on things like emotional significance, repetition, novelty, context, personalization, and revisits over time.
Autonomous initiation, so Ava can sometimes decide to speak first instead of only responding.
Inactivity-based initiation, so she only considers speaking after enough quiet time has passed.
Thought selection for initiation from curiosity questions, goals, reflections, and now recurring conversation themes.
Cooldown systems to reduce spam, including global cooldown, topic cooldown, pending-response tracking, and added ignored-initiation backoff.
Semantic topic suppression, so she is less likely to bring up the same thing again with only slightly different wording.
Per-kind pacing, so curiosity, reflection, and check-in style initiations can happen at different rates.
Contextual awareness of repeated themes, so she can notice patterns in what keeps coming up.
Pattern recognition, so recurring moods or topics can influence whether she checks in or suggests a shift.
Social awareness, so typing, short replies, no engagement, and signs of busyness can affect whether she chooses to speak.
Energy management, so her tendency to initiate can rise or fall based on how interactions have been going.
Confidence guidance, so after being ignored multiple times she can back off or ask whether she should wait or focus elsewhere.
Emotional meaning system, where emotions are no longer just labels but have meanings, focuses, tone tendencies, and style contributions.
Style blending, so combinations of emotions can produce outward styles like playful, caring, focused, reflective, cautious, or low-energy.
Behavior modifiers driven by emotional state, affecting warmth, humor, assertiveness, caution, initiative, and conversational depth.
More explicit style guidance in the prompt, so mood affects normal reply style more reliably instead of being only implied.
Emotion-driven initiative scoring, so her emotional state can noticeably influence whether and how she self-initiates.
Expression sensing was planned/added as a soft-context layer, so webcam expressions can act as hints rather than hard facts.
Workbench/self-action support, so Ava can write to allowed workbench files and save memories through hidden action blocks.

Big-picture summary:

you moved from a mostly reactive assistant toward an actual agent-like system with memory, reflection, goals, emotional style, presence detection, and self-initiated conversation.
